name: nova-scheduler-filters

metadata.name: nova-scheduler-filters
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Additional filters for the OpenStack Nova scheduler

documentation:
# Nova Scheduler Filters

This project contains a set of filters for the Nova scheduler. These filters
are intended to be used in conjunction with the default filters that ship with
Nova.

The goal of those filters are to provide additional filtering capabilities
that can be co-installed with any version of Nova until the features are
upstreamed.

## Failure domain

The failure domain filter is a filter that can be used to spread instances
across different failure domains. A failure domain is a set of compute nodes
that are expected to fail together. For example, compute nodes in the same
rack or in the same power distribution unit (PDU) are expected to fail
together.

Historically, some users have used the availability zone feature to spread
instances across failure domains. However, the availability zone feature is
not designed for this purpose and it has some limitations. There is also
a need to spread instances across failure domains within the same availability
zone.

The failure domain is defined as a metadata on host aggregates. The key of
the metadata is `failure_domain`. The value of the metadata is a string that
identifies the failure domain. The failure domain filter will spread instances
across different failure domains. It is up to the operator to define the
failure domains.

classes.name: nova_scheduler_filters.failure_domain_filter.FailureDomainFilter

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.failure_domain_filter.FailureDomainFilter._get_failure_domain
documentation:
Retrieve the failure domain from the host's aggregates, return None if
the host does not belong to any failure domain.

Filter to ensure instances in a server group are placed in different
failure domains.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_no_scheduler_hint
documentation: Ensures that the filter passes if no scheduler hint is provided.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_different_failure_domain_false
documentation: Ensures that the filter passes if the scheduler hint is set to false.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_no_instance_group
documentation:
Ensures that the filter passes if the instance is not part of a
server group.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_host_not_in_failure_domain
documentation:
Ensures that the filter fails if the host does not belong to any
failure domain.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_empty_server_group_with_no_failure_domain
documentation:
Ensures that the filter fails if the server group is empty and the
host does not belong to any failure domain.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_empty_server_group_with_failure_domain
documentation:
Ensures that the filter passes if the server group is empty and the
host belongs to a failure domain.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_different_failure_domain
documentation:
Ensures that the filter passes if the host belongs to a different
failure domain compared to other hosts in the server group.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_same_failure_domain
documentation:
Ensures that the filter fails if the host belongs to the same failure
domain as another host in the server group.

methods.name: nova_scheduler_filters.tests.unit.test_failure_domain_filter.TestFailureDomainFilter.test_other_host_not_in_failure_domain
documentation:
Ensures that the filter passes if the other host in the server group
does not belong to any failure domain.
